title: viburnum tomentosum var. plicatum
intro: Viburnum tomentosum var. plicatum, also known as the wrinkled-leaved leatherleaf viburnum, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive wrinkled leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Viburnum tomentosum var. plicatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
